# Overview [CURRENT] ‚Äî package: overview ‚Äî rendered: 2026-04-13T22:04:23Z

Three sibling data-layer pipelines ‚Äî filter, propagation, and transformation ‚Äî control what information reaches agents, observe what agents produce, and reformat controller responses. All three share the same layer hierarchy (`FilterLayerCatalog`), the same internal-automation guard (preventing recursive AI calls), and the same AI execution pattern (`AgentLlmExecutor.runDirect()`), but they fire at different points in the request lifecycle and serve distinct purposes.

# The Three Pipelines at a Glance [CURRENT] ‚Äî package: the-three-pipelines-at-a-glance ‚Äî rendered: 2026-04-13T22:04:23Z

| Dimension | Filter | Propagation | Transformation |
|---|---|---|---|
| Purpose | Gate/modify prompt contributor text or GraphEvents before emission | Observe agent I/O and create escalation items for controller review | Reformat REST response bodies before they reach the API consumer |
| Trigger point | `FilteredPromptContributorAdapter` (before LLM) or `ControllerEventFilterIntegration` (before event stream) | `PropagateActionRequestDecorator` (before action) and `PropagateActionResultDecorator` (after action) | `ControllerResponseTransformationAdvice` (Spring `ResponseBodyAdvice`, on every controller response) |
| Data flow | PASSTHROUGH / TRANSFORMED / DROPPED (can block or mutate) | Read-only observation; creates `PropagationItem` as side effect | Mutates the HTTP response body |
| Output | `FilterDecisionRecordEntity` | `PropagationItemEntity` + `PropagationRecordEntity` | `TransformationRecordEntity` |
| AI template | `filter/ai_filter.jinja` | `propagation/ai_propagator.jinja` | `transformation/ai_transformer.jinja` |

# Filter Evaluation End-to-End [CURRENT] ‚Äî package: filter-evaluation ‚Äî rendered: 2026-04-13T22:04:23Z

References: 52 files tracked (package: filter-evaluation) ‚Äî see `$.sections["Filter Evaluation End-to-End"]` in .metadata/HEAD

```
Prompt contributor text (or GraphEvent)
 ‚îÇ
 ‚ñº
Entry point: PathFilterIntegration.applyTextPathFilters()
             or ControllerEventFilterIntegration.applyFilters()
 ‚îÇ
 ‚ñº
FilterExecutionService.executeFilters(layerId, payload, ctx, source, allowedKinds)
 ‚îÇ
 ‚îú‚îÄ 1. POLICY DISCOVERY ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ PolicyDiscoveryService.getActivePoliciesByLayer(layerId)
 ‚îÇ      walks ancestor chain via LayerService.getEffectiveLayers()
 ‚îÇ      keeps only policies with active PolicyLayerBinding to any effective layer
 ‚îÇ      sorts by priority (ascending), then registrationId as tie-breaker
 ‚îÇ
 ‚îú‚îÄ 2. BINDING MATCH ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ for each policy, matchesBinding() checks:
 ‚îÇ      matchOn (PROMPT_CONTRIBUTOR or GRAPH_EVENT) must align with FilterSource type
 ‚îÇ      matcherKey (NAME or TEXT) + matcherType (EQUALS, REGEX, CONTAINS) + matcherText
 ‚îÇ
 ‚îú‚îÄ 3. DISPATCH ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ switch on Filter sealed type:
 ‚îÇ      ‚îÇ
 ‚îÇ      ‚îú‚îÄ PathFilter (deterministic):
 ‚îÇ      ‚îÇ    executor.apply(payload, context) ‚Üí List<Instruction>
 ‚îÇ      ‚îÇ    DispatchingInterpreter applies instructions by PathType
 ‚îÇ      ‚îÇ    (REGEX ‚Üí RegexInterpreter, MARKDOWN_PATH ‚Üí MarkdownInterpreter, JSON_PATH ‚Üí JsonPathInterpreter)
 ‚îÇ      ‚îÇ
 ‚îÇ      ‚îî‚îÄ AiPathFilter (LLM-powered):
 ‚îÇ           AiFilterTool (hydrated by Spring autowiring after JSON deserialization)
 ‚îÇ           ‚Üí AgentLlmExecutor.runDirect() ‚Üí AiFilterResult with List<Instruction>
 ‚îÇ           ‚Üí same DispatchingInterpreter
 ‚îÇ
 ‚îú‚îÄ 4. DECISION RECORDING ‚îÄ‚îÄ FilterDecisionRecordEntity persisted
 ‚îÇ      (action = PASSTHROUGH / TRANSFORMED / DROPPED / ERROR)
 ‚îÇ
 ‚îî‚îÄ 5. RETURN ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ FilterResult<T> with possibly mutated payload
        if any policy DROPs, loop breaks early
```

# Propagation: Observer Pattern on Agent I/O [CURRENT] ‚Äî package: propagation-observer ‚Äî rendered: 2026-04-13T22:04:23Z

References: 39 files tracked (package: propagation-observer) ‚Äî see `$.sections["Propagation: Observer Pattern on Agent I/O"]` in .metadata/HEAD

Propagators are **observers**, not gatekeepers ‚Äî they cannot block or alter the agent's execution path. Their job is to surface controller-relevant information.

**Hook points** (via the agent decorator chain):

- `PropagateActionRequestDecorator` (order 10000): fires before every agent action, calls `ActionRequestPropagationIntegration.propagate()` with `PropagatorMatchOn.ACTION_REQUEST`
- `PropagateActionResultDecorator` (order 10002): fires after every agent action, calls `ActionResponsePropagationIntegration.propagate()` with `PropagatorMatchOn.ACTION_RESPONSE`

**The propagation process:**

```
Agent decorator chain calls PropagateAction[Request|Result]Decorator
 ‚îÇ
 ‚îú‚îÄ Guard: FilterLayerCatalog.isInternalAutomationAction() ‚Äî prevents recursive AI propagation
 ‚îú‚îÄ Layer resolution: FilterLayerCatalog.resolveActionLayer(agentName, actionName, methodName)
 ‚îú‚îÄ Compact serialize: request.prettyPrint(PropagatorSerialization)
 ‚îÇ
 ‚ñº
PropagationExecutionService.execute(layerId, stage, payload, nodeId, actionName, operationContext)
 ‚îÇ
 ‚îú‚îÄ For each active propagator bound to the layer:
 ‚îÇ    ‚îú‚îÄ TextPropagator ‚Üí deterministic text processing
 ‚îÇ    ‚îî‚îÄ AiTextPropagator ‚Üí AgentLlmExecutor.runDirect() via AiPropagatorTool
 ‚îÇ
 ‚îú‚îÄ PropagationItemEntity created if not already present (correlation key dedup)
 ‚îú‚îÄ PropagationRecordEntity persisted (action = ITEM_CREATED / TRANSFORMED / PASSTHROUGH / FAILED)
 ‚îî‚îÄ Events.PropagationEvent published to EventBus
```

Auto-registration: at startup, `PropagatorRegistrationService.ensureAutoAiPropagatorsRegistered()` bootstraps an AI propagator for every action in `FilterLayerCatalog`.

# Transformation: Response Body Rewriting [CURRENT] ‚Äî package: transformation-rewriting ‚Äî rendered: 2026-04-13T22:04:23Z

References: 23 files tracked (package: transformation-rewriting) ‚Äî see `$.sections["Transformation: Response Body Rewriting"]` in .metadata/HEAD

```
REST controller response body
 ‚îÇ
 ‚ñº
ControllerResponseTransformationAdvice (Spring ResponseBodyAdvice)
 ‚îÇ fires on every @RestController in com.hayden.multiagentide.controller
 ‚îÇ
 ‚ñº
ControllerEndpointTransformationIntegration
 ‚îÇ layerId = FilterLayerCatalog.CONTROLLER (always)
 ‚îÇ
 ‚ñº
TransformerExecutionService.execute()
 ‚îÇ
 ‚îú‚îÄ For each active transformer bound to the layer:
 ‚îÇ    ‚îú‚îÄ TextTransformer ‚Üí deterministic text rewriting
 ‚îÇ    ‚îî‚îÄ AiTextTransformer ‚Üí AgentLlmExecutor.runDirect() via AiTransformerTool
 ‚îÇ
 ‚îî‚îÄ TransformationRecordEntity persisted
```

# The Layer Hierarchy [CURRENT] ‚Äî package: layer-hierarchy ‚Äî rendered: 2026-04-13T22:04:23Z

References: 12 files tracked (package: layer-hierarchy) ‚Äî see `$.sections["The Layer Hierarchy"]` in .metadata/HEAD

All three pipelines bind to the same static layer tree defined in `FilterLayerCatalog`:

```
controller (depth 0)
 ‚îú‚îÄ controller-ui-event-poll (depth 1)
 ‚îú‚îÄ workflow-agent (depth 1)
 ‚îÇ    ‚îú‚îÄ discovery-dispatch-subagent (depth 2)
 ‚îÇ    ‚îú‚îÄ planning-dispatch-subagent (depth 2)
 ‚îÇ    ‚îú‚îÄ ticket-dispatch-subagent (depth 2)
 ‚îÇ    ‚îî‚îÄ [per-action layers: workflow-agent/runDiscoveryAgent, etc.] (depth 3)
 ‚îú‚îÄ interrupt-service (depth 1)
 ‚îú‚îÄ worktree-auto-commit (depth 1)
 ‚îú‚îÄ ai-filter (depth 1)
 ‚îú‚îÄ ai-propagator (depth 1)
 ‚îî‚îÄ ai-transformer (depth 1)
```

**Policy binding propagation**: at registration time, `PolicyRegistrationService.propagateBindings()` duplicates bindings to descendant layers (if `isInheritable=true`) or parent layers (if `isPropagatedToParent=true`). At execution time, `PolicyDiscoveryService` independently walks the ancestor chain so a policy at `workflow-agent` also fires for `workflow-agent/coordinateWorkflow`.

# The AI Execution Pattern (Shared by All Three) [CURRENT] ‚Äî package: ai-execution-pattern ‚Äî rendered: 2026-04-13T22:04:23Z

References: 7 files tracked (package: ai-execution-pattern) ‚Äî see `$.sections["The AI Execution Pattern (Shared by All Three)"]` in .metadata/HEAD

All three AI-powered variants follow the same pattern:

1. **Hydration**: The tool (`AiFilterTool`, `AiPropagatorTool`, `AiTransformerTool`) is stored as JSON in the database. After Jackson deserialization, `@JsonIgnore` fields (like `agentLlmExecutor`) are null. `AiFilterToolHydration.hydrate()` calls `applicationContext.getAutowireCapableBeanFactory().autowireBean(tool)` to inject Spring beans.

2. **Context assembly**: A `PromptContext` is built with the appropriate `agentType` (AI_FILTER / AI_PROPAGATOR / AI_TRANSFORMER), template name, and model map (payload, policyId, registrarPrompt). A `ToolContext` is decorated by the standard chain.

3. **`DirectExecutorArgs`**: Bundles prompt context, tool context, template model, `OperationContext`, and response class (`AiFilterResult` / `AiPropagatorResult` / `AiTransformerResult`).

4. **LLM call**: `agentLlmExecutor.runDirect(args)` renders the Jinja template, calls ACP, returns structured response.

5. **Recursion guard**: `FilterLayerCatalog.isInternalAutomationAction()` prevents the AI filter/propagator/transformer from triggering propagation on their own requests/results (roots: `ai-filter`, `ai-propagator`, `ai-transformer`).

6. **Session mode**: `AiFilterSessionResolver.resolveSessionKey()` picks the chat key ‚Äî `PER_INVOCATION` (fresh each time), `SAME_SESSION_FOR_ACTION`, `SAME_SESSION_FOR_ALL`, or `SAME_SESSION_FOR_AGENT`.
